summary: Resolved CI, validation, accessibility, BDD and Release Gate failures after shared GOV.UK x-include chrome changes while preserving the title-case service name ResearchOps Demo Suite.

casingDecision.serviceNameRule: Keep title case for service identity, header partials and page title suffixes.
casingDecision.headingRule: Assert paragraph case only for actual page and content headings, not for text inside the header service identity.

validatedGreen.head: 971ac1d6cc556e3cc0f5f6c9fbdaeb3264ebb7ee
workflows: Build and deploy agent documentation Pages

releaseGate.previousCause: Unit tests on the Release Gate merge ref saw stale rendered files and brittle title/heading assertions.
resolvedBy: Preserved ResearchOps Demo Suite in service identity assertions.
Scoped paragraph-case assertions to page and content headings.
Aligned checked-in rendered representative pages with the GOV.UK layout source.
Changed the start overview action test to assert link target semantics rather than attribute order.

residualRisk: The wider GOV.UK migration is still incomplete by design. Existing non-spike routes still depend on legacy shared GOV.UK imitation CSS and shared chrome contracts.
